# Contract Generated by GPT-5.2 (Latest)

**Model ID:** `gpt-5.2`

---

## Freelance Service Agreement (California)

### Preamble

This Freelance Service Agreement (this “Agreement”) is entered into as of January 15, 2025 (the “Effective Date”), by and between TechStartup Inc., a company (“Client”), and Chen Creative Studio, a company (“Freelancer”). Client and Freelancer may be referred to herein individually as a “Party” and collectively as the “Parties.”

### Recitals

WHEREAS, Client desires to retain Freelancer to provide professional design services in connection with Client’s brand refresh project; and
WHEREAS, Freelancer represents that it has the experience and ability to perform such services; and
WHEREAS, the Parties desire to set forth the terms and conditions under which Freelancer will provide such services to Client.

NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants and promises herein, the Parties agree as follows:

---

## Clauses (17 total)

### Engagement; Scope of Services

*Type: standard*

1.1 Engagement. Client hereby engages Freelancer, and Freelancer accepts such engagement, to perform the services described in this Agreement.

1.2 Project. The project is “Brand Refresh 2025,” consisting of a complete visual identity redesign including: (i) logo; (ii) color palette; (iii) typography system; and (iv) a brand guidelines document, together with the deliverables set forth in Section 2 (the “Services”).

1.3 Standard of Performance. Freelancer will perform the Services in a professional and workmanlike manner consistent with industry standards.

### Deliverables; Schedule; Acceptance

*Type: standard*

2.1 Deliverables and Milestones. Freelancer will provide the following deliverables (each, a “Deliverable”) in accordance with the schedule below (the “Schedule”):

(a) Logo concepts (3 options) — due January 30, 2025 — Milestone Fee: $2,000
(b) Selected logo refinement — due February 15, 2025 — Milestone Fee: $1,500
(c) Brand guidelines document — due March 1, 2025 — Milestone Fee: $2,500
(d) Asset package delivery — due March 15, 2025 — Milestone Fee: $2,500

2.2 Deadline. The overall project deadline is March 15, 2025, subject to Section 6 (Client Responsibilities; Delays) and Section 12 (Change Orders).

2.3 Review and Acceptance. Within five (5) business days after delivery of each Deliverable, Client will either (i) accept the Deliverable in writing (including by email), or (ii) provide a written list of nonconformities describing how the Deliverable materially fails to conform to the requirements of this Agreement. If Client does not provide notice within the review period, the Deliverable will be deemed accepted.

2.4 Cure. If Client provides a timely notice of nonconformity, Freelancer will use commercially reasonable efforts to correct the nonconformity and re-deliver the Deliverable for review.

### Fees; Deposit; Payment Schedule; Late Payments

*Type: standard*

3.1 Total Fee. Client will pay Freelancer a total of Eight Thousand Five Hundred Dollars ($8,500) (the “Total Fee”), inclusive of the milestone amounts listed in Section 2.1.

3.2 Deposit. A deposit of Two Thousand Five Hundred Fifty Dollars ($2,550) (the “Deposit”) is due upon execution of this Agreement and will be applied to the Total Fee.

3.3 Milestone Payments. Following payment of the Deposit, Client will pay the remaining balance via milestone payments aligned to Deliverables:

(a) Logo concepts milestone fee: $2,000 (less applicable portion of Deposit credit, if applied by Client and Freelancer to this milestone)
(b) Selected logo refinement milestone fee: $1,500
(c) Brand guidelines document milestone fee: $2,500
(d) Asset package delivery milestone fee: $2,500

Unless the Parties agree in writing to allocate the Deposit across milestones, the Deposit will be applied to the final invoice.

3.4 Invoicing; Due Date. Freelancer may invoice the Deposit upon execution and each milestone upon delivery of the corresponding Deliverable (or upon deemed acceptance under Section 2.3). All invoices are due net fifteen (15) days from invoice date.

3.5 Late Payments. Amounts not paid when due will accrue interest at the lesser of (i) one and one-half percent (1.5%) per month, or (ii) the maximum rate permitted by applicable law, plus reasonable costs of collection, including reasonable attorneys’ fees, to the extent permitted by law.

3.6 Suspension for Nonpayment. If any undisputed amount is more than ten (10) days past due, Freelancer may suspend work upon five (5) business days’ written notice until payment is received. The Schedule will be extended day-for-day for the period of suspension.

### Revisions Included; Out-of-Scope Revisions

*Type: standard*

4.1 Included Revision Rounds. The Total Fee includes up to three (3) rounds of revisions in total for the project. A “revision round” means one consolidated set of Client feedback delivered at one time per Deliverable stage.

4.2 Consolidated Feedback. Client will provide feedback in a single, consolidated communication per revision round where reasonably possible.

4.3 Additional Revisions. Revisions beyond the included three (3) rounds, or revisions requested after acceptance of a Deliverable, are out of scope and will be billed at Freelancer’s then-current hourly rate of $175/hour (or such other rate agreed in writing) in increments of 0.25 hours, subject to a written estimate and Client approval before commencement.

4.4 No Implied Unlimited Revisions. The Parties acknowledge that the Services are not an unlimited revision arrangement.

### Client Responsibilities; Cooperation

*Type: standard*

5.1 Timely Inputs. Client will provide timely access to information, materials, brand assets, decision-makers, and feedback reasonably necessary for Freelancer to perform the Services.

5.2 Single Point of Contact. Client will designate Sarah Johnson (Marketing Director) as the primary point of contact, with authority to provide direction and approvals.

5.3 Third-Party Access. If access to Client systems or third-party platforms is required, Client will provide or procure such access.

5.4 Accuracy of Materials. Client represents that materials provided by Client do not infringe third-party rights and that Client has all necessary rights to provide them to Freelancer.

### Schedule Changes; Delays

*Type: standard*

6.1 Delay by Client. If Client delays the project by failing to provide required information, feedback, approvals, or access, the Schedule and deadline will be extended by the length of the delay, and Freelancer will not be responsible for missed deadlines caused by such delay.

6.2 Delay by Freelancer. If Freelancer anticipates missing a deadline for reasons within Freelancer’s control, Freelancer will promptly notify Client and propose a revised timeline.

6.3 No Guaranteed Market Outcomes. Client acknowledges that design services do not guarantee specific marketing, sales, or business outcomes.

### Intellectual Property; License; Ownership

*Type: standard*

7.1 Definitions. “Work Product” means all deliverables and other work created by Freelancer specifically for Client under this Agreement, including the final logo, final brand guidelines, and final asset package, excluding Freelancer Background IP (defined below).

7.2 Freelancer Background IP. “Freelancer Background IP” means pre-existing materials, tools, templates, methodologies, software, fonts, stock assets, and know-how owned or licensed by Freelancer and not created specifically for Client under this Agreement.

7.3 Ownership Transfer Upon Full Payment. Subject to Client’s payment in full of all amounts due under this Agreement, and except for Freelancer Background IP and Third-Party Materials (defined below), Freelancer hereby assigns to Client all right, title, and interest in and to the final, accepted Work Product.

7.4 License Pending Payment. Until full payment is received, Client receives a limited, revocable, non-transferable license to use the Work Product solely for internal review.

7.5 Third-Party Materials. Certain elements may be subject to third-party license terms, including but not limited to fonts, stock photos, and mockups (“Third-Party Materials”). Freelancer will identify known Third-Party Materials included in the Work Product. Client is responsible for obtaining and maintaining any required licenses unless otherwise agreed in writing.

7.6 Moral Rights. To the extent permitted under applicable law, Freelancer waives any “moral rights” or equivalent rights in the Work Product. If any such rights cannot be waived, Freelancer agrees not to assert them in a manner that interferes with Client’s use of the Work Product.

7.7 Portfolio Use. Unless Client provides written objection for confidential reasons, Freelancer may display non-confidential portions of the Work Product in Freelancer’s portfolio and marketing materials after public launch by Client, or ninety (90) days after final delivery, whichever occurs first, provided Freelancer does not disclose Client Confidential Information.

### Confidentiality

*Type: standard*

8.1 Confidential Information. “Confidential Information” means any non-public information disclosed by a Party to the other Party that is designated as confidential or that reasonably should be understood to be confidential, including business plans, marketing plans, product information, and brand strategy.

8.2 Obligations. The receiving Party will (i) use Confidential Information only as necessary to perform or receive the Services; (ii) not disclose Confidential Information to any third party except to its employees/contractors with a need to know and bound by confidentiality obligations at least as protective as those herein; and (iii) protect Confidential Information using reasonable care.

8.3 Exclusions. Confidential Information does not include information that the receiving Party can demonstrate: (i) is or becomes publicly available through no fault of the receiving Party; (ii) was known to the receiving Party prior to disclosure; (iii) is independently developed without use of the disclosing Party’s Confidential Information; or (iv) is received from a third party without breach of a duty of confidentiality.

8.4 Compelled Disclosure. If required by law or court order, the receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information provided it gives prompt notice (to the extent legally permitted) and cooperates with reasonable efforts to seek protective treatment.

8.5 Return/Destruction. Upon request, the receiving Party will return or destroy the disclosing Party’s Confidential Information, except that one archival copy may be retained for legal/compliance purposes.

### Independent Contractor; No Employment Relationship

*Type: standard*

9.1 Independent Contractor. Freelancer is an independent contractor and not an employee, agent, joint venturer, or partner of Client. Freelancer has no authority to bind Client.

9.2 Taxes and Benefits. Freelancer is solely responsible for all taxes, withholdings, and other statutory or contractual obligations of any sort, including workers’ compensation and unemployment insurance, relating to compensation paid to Freelancer.

9.3 Control of Work. Freelancer retains the right to control the manner and means of performing the Services, consistent with Client’s requirements for the Deliverables.

9.4 California Compliance. The Parties intend this Agreement to comply with applicable California law governing independent contractors, including the extent applicable, California Labor Code and related regulations.

### Representations and Warranties

*Type: standard*

10.1 Mutual. Each Party represents and warrants that: (i) it has full power and authority to enter into and perform this Agreement; and (ii) this Agreement constitutes a legal, valid, and binding obligation.

10.2 By Freelancer. Freelancer represents and warrants that the Work Product will be original to Freelancer (except for Third-Party Materials and Client-provided materials) and, to Freelancer’s knowledge, will not infringe any U.S. intellectual property rights of third parties.

10.3 Disclaimer. EXCEPT AS EXPRESSLY SET FORTH IN THIS AGREEMENT, FREELANCER DISCLAIMS ALL WARRANTIES, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, INCLUDING ANY IMPLIED WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY, FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE, AND NON-INFRINGEMENT.

### Indemnification

*Type: standard*

11.1 By Freelancer. Freelancer will indemnify, defend, and hold harmless Client and its officers, directors, employees, and agents from and against any third-party claims, damages, liabilities, costs, and expenses (including reasonable attorneys’ fees) arising out of an allegation that the final, accepted Work Product (excluding Client materials and Third-Party Materials) infringes such third party’s U.S. copyright or trademark, provided that Client: (i) promptly notifies Freelancer in writing; (ii) allows Freelancer to control the defense and settlement; and (iii) reasonably cooperates.

11.2 By Client. Client will indemnify, defend, and hold harmless Freelancer from and against any third-party claims, damages, liabilities, costs, and expenses (including reasonable attorneys’ fees) arising out of or relating to: (i) materials or instructions supplied by Client; (ii) Client’s use of the Work Product in combination with materials not provided by Freelancer; or (iii) Client’s breach of this Agreement.

11.3 Mitigation/Remedy. If a Deliverable is subject to an infringement claim, Freelancer may, at its option: (i) procure the right for Client to continue using it; (ii) modify it so it becomes non-infringing while substantially preserving functionality and intent; or (iii) refund amounts paid for the infringing portion of the Work Product upon return/cessation of use. This Section states Client’s exclusive remedies for IP infringement claims.

### Change Orders; Additional Services

*Type: negotiable*

12.1 Change Requests. Client may request changes to the scope, Deliverables, or Schedule. Any material change must be documented in a written change order signed (including electronically) by both Parties describing the change, impact on fees, and timeline.

12.2 Out-of-Scope Work. Work not expressly included in Section 1.2 and Section 2 is out of scope and will require a change order or be billed at the hourly rate stated in Section 4.3.

12.3 Expedited Work. If Client requests expedited delivery, the Parties will agree in writing on any rush fees and revised Schedule before Freelancer is obligated to perform expedited work.

### Term; Termination

*Type: standard*

13.1 Term. This Agreement begins on the Effective Date and continues until completion of the Services and payment of all amounts due, unless earlier terminated as set forth herein.

13.2 Termination for Convenience. Either Party may terminate this Agreement for convenience upon seven (7) days’ written notice.

13.3 Termination for Cause. Either Party may terminate this Agreement immediately upon written notice if the other Party materially breaches this Agreement and fails to cure within five (5) business days after receipt of written notice describing the breach.

13.4 Effect of Termination. Upon termination: (i) Client will pay Freelancer for all Services performed and authorized costs incurred through the termination effective date, including any completed milestones, plus any non-cancelable commitments; (ii) if the Deposit exceeds amounts due for work performed, Freelancer will refund the difference within fifteen (15) days; and (iii) Freelancer will deliver to Client any Work Product completed and paid for as of the termination date.

13.5 Survival. Sections intended by their nature to survive (including confidentiality, IP, payment obligations, limitation of liability, dispute resolution, and general provisions) will survive termination.

### Limitation of Liability

*Type: standard*

TO THE MAXIMUM EXTENT PERMITTED UNDER CALIFORNIA LAW, IN NO EVENT WILL EITHER PARTY BE LIABLE FOR ANY INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, CONSEQUENTIAL, OR PUNITIVE DAMAGES (INCLUDING LOST PROFITS, REVENUE, OR BUSINESS INTERRUPTION) ARISING OUT OF OR RELATING TO THIS AGREEMENT, EVEN IF ADVISED OF THE POSSIBILITY OF SUCH DAMAGES. EXCEPT FOR (I) A PARTY’S INDEMNIFICATION OBLIGATIONS, (II) BREACH OF CONFIDENTIALITY, OR (III) CLIENT’S PAYMENT OBLIGATIONS, EACH PARTY’S TOTAL LIABILITY ARISING OUT OF OR RELATING TO THIS AGREEMENT WILL NOT EXCEED THE AMOUNTS PAID OR PAYABLE BY CLIENT TO FREELANCER UNDER THIS AGREEMENT.

### Dispute Resolution; Governing Law; Venue

*Type: standard*

15.1 Good Faith Negotiation. The Parties will attempt in good faith to resolve any dispute arising out of or relating to this Agreement through informal discussions between executives with authority to settle the dispute.

15.2 Mediation. If the dispute is not resolved within fifteen (15) days after a written request for negotiation, either Party may request non-binding mediation administered by a mutually agreed mediator in California. The Parties will share mediator fees equally.

15.3 Litigation; Venue. If mediation is unsuccessful or not pursued, either Party may bring an action in the state or federal courts located in the County of San Francisco, California, and each Party consents to the personal jurisdiction and venue of such courts.

15.4 Governing Law. This Agreement is governed by the laws of the State of California, without regard to conflict of law principles.

### Notices

*Type: standard*

All notices under this Agreement must be in writing and will be deemed given when delivered personally, sent by certified mail (return receipt requested), nationally recognized overnight courier, or email with confirmation of transmission, to the addresses set forth below (or to such other address a Party designates by notice).

Notices to Client:
TechStartup Inc.
Attn: Sarah Johnson, Marketing Director
[Client Address]
Email: [Client Email]

Notices to Freelancer:
Chen Creative Studio
Attn: Alex Chen, Senior Designer
[Freelancer Address]
Email: [Freelancer Email]

### Miscellaneous (Entire Agreement; Assignment; Severability; Waiver; Counterparts; Electronic Signatures)

*Type: standard*

17.1 Entire Agreement. This Agreement, including any exhibits or change orders signed by the Parties, constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties regarding its subject matter and supersedes all prior or contemporaneous agreements.

17.2 Amendment. Any amendment must be in writing and signed by both Parties.

17.3 Assignment. Neither Party may assign this Agreement without the prior written consent of the other Party, except that Client may assign to a successor in connection with a merger, acquisition, or sale of substantially all assets.

17.4 Severability. If any provision is held invalid or unenforceable, the remaining provisions will remain in full force.

17.5 Waiver. No waiver is effective unless in writing. A waiver of any breach is not a waiver of any other or subsequent breach.

17.6 Counterparts; Electronic Signatures. This Agreement may be executed in counterparts, each of which is deemed an original. Signatures transmitted electronically (e.g., PDF, DocuSign) will be deemed original and binding.

17.7 Force Majeure. Neither Party will be liable for delays or failure to perform due to causes beyond its reasonable control, including acts of God, natural disasters, labor disputes, internet outages, or governmental actions, provided the affected Party gives prompt notice and resumes performance as soon as practicable.
